isolation.boundary: Repo-local CLI tests only; no application source changes; no shared service restarts

evidence.observed: The targeted test passed and verifies ClaimNextRun moves a pending review_run to running while populating claimed_by, claimed_at, and started_at.

assertions.reason: Mock-backed scheduler integration coverage for pending-run pickup passed against a Docker-backed MySQL test database.

evidence.observed: Both subcases passed: the first retry used the configured base delay and a later retry was capped at the configured max delay while retry_count incremented and next_retry_at was set.

assertions.reason: The passing retry-backoff test confirms failed runs remain retryable with bounded exponential backoff scheduling.

evidence.observed: The concurrent-claim test passed and verified two simultaneously pending runs for different MR IDs in the same project were both claimed, with distinct run IDs and merge_request IDs.

assertions.reason: Passing concurrency coverage shows scheduler claims remain isolated per MR without cross-contaminating state.

evidence.observed: The recovery test passed after one retryable failure and one successful retry, verifying final status completed, retry_count remained 1, next_retry_at cleared, downstream writes executed once, and only one review_run row existed.

assertions.reason: This passing recovery test provides concrete evidence that retryable failures can complete on retry without duplicating downstream work.

evidence.observed: The multi-worker claim race test passed and verified exactly one worker claimed the pending run while the other received ErrNoClaimableRuns, leaving the stored run in running status with claimed_by set to the winning worker.

assertions.reason: The passing race test confirms single-claim behavior across worker instances.

summary: Executed the narrow scheduler test slice for the assigned CLI assertions. All five assigned assertions passed using mock-backed scheduler coverage in internal/scheduler/service_test.go.
